msg.description:
Sends a message to the player.
You can use @p to substitute their full player name (includes the +) or @nick to subtitute for a more natural version of their name (e.g. Mike_30+ becomes Mike)

cpemsg.description: Same as msg, but allows you to send to the other special chat fields in top right, bottom right, or center.

kill.description: Kills the player with an optional public death message.

cmd.description: Runs the given command with the given arguments.

tempblock.description: Shortcut for "cmd tempblock [args]". See /help tempblock for more info.

tempchunk.description: Shortcut for "cmd tempchunk [args]". See /help tempchunk for more info.

delay.description: Makes the script pause for the amount of milliseconds specified.

item.description: Gives an item to the player or takes an item from the player.

goto.description: Makes the script jump to the label you specify and does not come back, making it ideal for splitting paths.

call.description: Like goto, but once it reaches a "quit" in the [#label] called, instead of quitting, it will come back and run what comes after the call.

jump.description: This Action is identical to goto unless we are currently in a label that was ran using the call Action.

newthread.description: Like call, but allows the script to continue running without taking into account any of the delays in the label you called.

set.description: Sets the [value] of [package]. If you want to set the value of a package to the value of another package, you have to unwrap it in the value argument with { }.

setadd.description: Adds the second argument to the first argument.

setsub.description: Subtracts the second argument from the first argument.

setmul.description: Multiplies the second argument with the first argument.

setdiv.description: Divides the second argument from the first argument.

setmod.description: Sets the first argument to the remainder of an integer division between the first argument and the second argument.

setrandrange.description: Sets the first argument to a random integer that is within the range of the second and third args.

setrandrangedecimal.description: Sets the first argument to a random number that is within the range of the second and third args.

setrandlist.description: Sets the first argument to one of the given values that are separated by the | symbol.

setround.description: Rounds the value of the package to the nearest integer.

setroundup.description: Rounds the value of the package up to the next integer. For example, 1.1 becomes 2.

setrounddown.description: Rounds the value of the package down to the next integer. For example, 1.9 becomes 1.

setblockid.description: Sets the value of [package] to the ID of the block at the given [block coordinates].

show.description: Displays the value of all the packages given, for testing and debug purposes.

freeze.description: Freezes the player in place. They can still fall if mid-air or swim up and down in liquid or ladders, though.

look.description: Makes the player look at the given coordinates. They can move their camera afterwards.

stare.description: Forces the player to continually stare at the given coordinates. You can free their camera by not providing any coordinates to this action.

env.description: Temporarily changes env values for the player who runs the script.

motd.description: Sends an MOTD to the player to control hacks using hacks flags.

setspawn.description: Sets the spawn of the player to the coordinates provided.

reply.body: reply ${1:option number}|${2:text shown to player}|#${3:label to call if chosen}
reply.description: Sets up a reply option, which can be chosen by the player by typing [option number] in chat

reply_clear.description: Clears all current replies from being visible and useable (this includes silent replies)

replysilent.body: replysilent ${1:option number}|${2:text shown to player}|#${3:label to call if chosen}
replysilent.description: Identical to reply, with two exceptions: Does not notify the player that they should choose a response; Does not remind the player to choose a response if they chat while silent replies are active.

replysilent_clear.description: Clears all current replies from being visible and useable (this includes non-silent replies)

reach.description: Temporarily sets the player's reach distance, in blocks.

quit.description: Typically this tells the script to stop running.

terminate.description: This tells the script to stop running completely, even if we are in a nested label that was called from somewhere else using "call".

include.description: Similar to C "#include". All of the contents of [scriptname] will be copy-pasted into your script when it is compiled. Use os/[mapname] to include from an OS map.

using.description: Top-level option toggle. Write it once somewhere in your script (at the top is standard practice) to activate.

localmsg.description: Same as cpemsg, but sends to all players in the level similar to death messages and local chat. Use "chat" to send to normal chat.

menumsg.description: Same as cpemsg for announcements, but stays on the screen instead of disappearing after 5 seconds.

cs_me.description: Plays a chatsound for this player, as if coming from self. The player must have chatsounds plugin v3.13 or newer.

cs_pos.description: Plays a chatsound for this player, as if coming from the given coordinates.

cs_bot.description: Plays a chatsound for this player, as if coming from the given bot or tempbot.

setpow.description: Performs a math "power of" operation on [package] and sets that as its new value.

setsin.description: Calculates sine of [number or package] and inserts it into [package].

setcos.description: Calculates cosine of [number or package] and inserts it into [package].

settan.description: Calculates the tangent of [number or package] (radians) and inserts it into [package].

setarcsin.description: Calculates the angle (in radians) whose sine is equal to [number or package].

setarccos.description: Calculates the angle (in radians) whose cosine is equal to [number or package].

setarctan.description: Calculates the angle (in radians) whose tangent is equal to [number or package].

setradtodeg.description: Converts [number or package] from radians into degrees.

setdegtorad.description: Converts [number or package] from degrees into radians.

setsqrt.description: Calculates the square root of [number or package] and inserts it into [package].

setfromhexcolor.description: Converts [hexColor] into integer red, green, and blue components in [package][0..2].

settohexcolor.description: Calculates the hex code based on the given [colorStructure] (three packages with [0..2] indices) and inserts it into [package].

setlength.description: Like setsplit, but just inserts the resulting length into [package].Length.

setsplit.description: Copies the contents of [package], then splits them up into a new set of indexed packages.

setsimilarity.description: Calculates the similarity of [phrase A] and [phrase B] (0 to 1) and inserts the result into [package]. Phrases can be packages or quote-enclosed literals.

setdirvector.description: Sets the value of x, y, and z packages to a 3D direction vector based on yaw and pitch in degrees.

setblockmessage.description: Sets the value of [package] to the message of the message block at the given coordinates (or nothing if no message exists).

getzonemotd.description: Sets the value of [package] to the MOTD of the named zone. Leaving the second argument empty gives the MOTD of the level instead.

setdeathspawn.description: Changes where the player will spawn after dying.

allowmbrepeat.description: Allows the next walk-through MB to be triggered, even if it's the same as the previously touched MB.

definehotkey.description: Allows the player to run the #input label by pressing a key. Modifiers can be any combination of ctrl, shift, alt, async.

undefinehotkey.description: Removes a previously defined hotkey. Matching modifiers must be included.

placeblock.description: Places blocks in the map. Unlike tempblock, these are permanently placed just like editing the map for real.

placemessageblock.description: Places message blocks in the map. If no message is provided, places a normal block and removes the message at the coordinates.

changemodel.description: Temporarily changes the player's model for the current world. Only works if the MOTD has one or more models forced with model=…. Run with no arguments to reset.

changeskin.description: Temporarily changes the player's skin for the current world. Other players see it as well. Run with no arguments to reset.

gui.description: Change properties of the player's gui to achieve cinematic effects. Use "gui reset" to restore defaults.

hasplugin.description: Checks if the player has [plugin name] and gives [package] a value of true if they have it, otherwise false. <version> is optional.

clickevent_register.description: Registers a label that will be called whenever the player clicks. Only works when map buildable and deletable are OFF.

clickevent_unregister.description: Unregisters the previously registered click event label.

chatevent_register.description: Registers two labels that will be called whenever the player chats. Not allowed in os scripts.

chatevent_unregister.description: Unregisters the previously registered chat event.

zonechangedevent_register.description: Registers a label that will be called whenever the player changes zones. runArg1 will be the name of the newly entered zone.

zonechangedevent_unregister.description: Unregisters the previously registered zonechangedevent label.

error.description: Force the script to error with [message] as the reason. Also terminates the script. Useful when data or args haven't been setup correctly.

boost.description: Shortcut for "cmd boost [args]". See /help boost for more info. Has faster performance than calling via cmd.

effect.description: Shortcut for "cmd effect [args]". See /help effect for more info. Has faster performance than calling via cmd.
